campus: Genoa (historical centre, medieval complex of Sant'Agostino)
admission_type: open access with curricular-requirements check and a personal-readiness assessment (no competitive ranking); a portfolio is central to the assessment

academic_requirements.summary: A first-cycle degree in architecture/architectural sciences (or recognised equivalent) with the required curricular credits, followed by a verification of adequacy of personal preparation in which the portfolio plays a central role.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A degree in class L-17 (Architectural Sciences) or class 4 (Architectural and Building Engineering Sciences); a three-year university degree allowing recognition of the required L-17 credits; or a five-year single-cycle degree (class 4/S or LM-4) when pursuing a second degree.
academic_requirements.required_credits_cfu: At least 108 CFU in the educational activities considered indispensable for class L-17 Architectural Sciences.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Architecture and architectural-engineering background; the programme deepens Italian architectural design culture with a central focus on architectural Composition.
academic_requirements.minimum_gpa_or_threshold: UniGeApply states a minimum Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 70% of the maximum obtainable for non-EU applicants assessed through the international pre-evaluation portal. The course page does not publish a separate numeric GPA gate. [uncertain]
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Holders of foreign qualifications are assessed for equivalence to the required class/credits; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through the UniGeApply pre-evaluation portal.

language_requirements.required_level: English proficiency at minimum B2 level.
accepted_proofs: A certificate from a recognised body (e.g. Cambridge, IELTS, TOEFL, PET).
A university certification (e.g. confirming the prior degree was taught in English / medium of instruction).
Passing the University of Genoa's B2-level English test (CLAT).
A letter from the university confirming an English-taught degree.
exemption_rules: On UniGeApply the language certificate is noted as optional for students from countries with ongoing conflicts; otherwise a B2 certificate or MOI certificate is required. [uncertain]
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified at the application/pre-evaluation stage (uploaded on UniGeApply for non-EU applicants).

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of adequacy of graduate personal readiness - a portfolio-based documentary assessment (no written entrance exam).
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants found eligible on curricular requirements, who must then take the personal-readiness assessment.
entry_exam_or_test.format: A special committee reviews submitted documents and the Master's Degree Coordinator evaluates the CV and the portfolio, which plays a central role in the application. UniGeApply indicates no remote interview/test is required for non-EU applicants.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Pre-enrolment is mandatory to participate in the verification; dates follow the course procedure. [uncertain]
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Outcomes are 'admitted', 'admitted under condition', or 'not admitted'.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Local pre-enrolment/online enrolment on UniGe online services generally opens in April each year and follows the UniGe enrolment calendar after a positive readiness assessment. [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu.notes: EU/resident applicants are assessed via the personal-readiness verification (portfolio review) and are not competitively ranked. The course page does not publish a separate programme-specific first-year EU application deadline, so the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window is used.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After a positive UniGeApply pre-evaluation, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment (Universitaly window 4 May 2026 to 19 June 2026) and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: No programme-specific early closure was found for this master in the official sources reviewed. [uncertain]
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session and the UniGeApply portal; an applicant may submit up to two assessment requests.

required_documents.notes: Mandatory for all UniGeApply master's applicants; reviewed by the Course Coordinator.
Plays a central role in the application; evaluated by the Master's Degree Coordinator during the personal-readiness verification.
Proof of the prior first-cycle qualification (class L-17 / class 4 or equivalent).

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (list of exams with credits and grades, and weighted average / CGPA)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the 108 CFU curricular requirement and the CGPA threshold (70% for non-EU applicants).

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or Medium-of-Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: Demonstrates English at B2; the language certificate is noted as optional for students from countries with ongoing conflicts.
Passport is the only accepted identity document for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply (photo as JPG, supporting documents as PDF up to 2 MB).

source_quotes.quote: Architectural Composition - Teaching Language English - Class LM-4 R (Architecture and Architectural Engineering) - Genova (Sant'Agostino), 120 CFU
addressed to foreign and Italian students interested to study and work in an international context
108 CFU in educational activities indispensable for class L-17 Architectural Sciences

English proficiency at minimum B2 level. Acceptable proofs include certificates (Cambridge, IELTS, TOEFL, PET), university certification, passing UniGe's B2-level English test, or a letter confirming an English-taught degree.
Portfolio (described as playing a central role in the application) ... Results are categorized as: 'admitted,' 'admitted under condition' or 'not admitted'.
Architectural Composition - Minimum CGPA: 70% - Remote interview/test: No - Portfolio; English certificate (or MOI); optional language certificate for students from countries with ongoing conflicts

uncertainty_notes: Per the task note, architecture masters can be restricted-access: confirmed there is NO competitive selection ranking - admission is open-access subject to a curricular check plus a portfolio-based personal-readiness verification (outcomes admitted / admitted under condition / not admitted). The portfolio is central and is the de facto selection instrument.
UniGeApply states no remote interview/test is required and a minimum CGPA of 70% for non-EU applicants; the course page itself does not publish a numeric GPA gate.
The English B2 language certificate is required, but on UniGeApply it is noted as optional for applicants from countries with ongoing conflicts.
No programme-specific fall-session early closure was found; institution-wide UniGeApply windows were used.
First-year EU application deadline reflects the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window; the course page does not publish a programme-specific first-year EU deadline.
